days.title: Arrival in Seville
days.morning: Fly into Seville (SVQ). Pick up rental car — you won't need it in the city, park it at the hotel. Walk to the **Cathedral and Giralda tower** — the largest Gothic cathedral in the world.
days.afternoon: **Real Alcázar** — a living royal palace with stunning Mudéjar architecture. Book timed entry online. The gardens alone are worth an hour.
days.evening: Tapas crawl in **El Arenal** neighbourhood. Order montaditos, jamón ibérico, and a cold Cruzcampo. Seville's tapas culture means small plates come free with drinks in many bars.
days.gem: The Alcázar rooftop terrace is included in the ticket but almost nobody goes up — views over the cathedral and old city are extraordinary.

days.title: Seville Deep Dive
days.theme: flamenco and the old Jewish quarter
days.morning: **Barrio Santa Cruz** — the old Jewish quarter, a labyrinth of whitewashed alleys, orange trees, and hidden plazas. Get lost intentionally.
days.afternoon: **Museo del Baile Flamenco** — the best flamenco museum in Spain. Evening show tickets available here too (book ahead).
days.evening: Live **flamenco tablao** show — Casa de la Memoria or Tablao El Arenal. Raw, intimate, nothing like the tourist versions elsewhere.
days.gem: Plaza de Santa Cruz has an iron cross in the centre that's actually a lamppost designed to look ancient — and the square has no cars, ever.

days.title: Seville → Córdoba
days.morning: Drive Seville to **Córdoba** (145km, 1h40). Check in and walk immediately to the **Mezquita-Catedral** — one of the most extraordinary buildings in the world. A cathedral built inside a mosque inside a Roman temple.
days.afternoon: **Judería** (Jewish quarter) and **Calleja de las Flores** — a tiny alley so pretty it's been photographed millions of times. Still worth seeing in person.
days.evening: Dinner in Córdoba — try **rabo de toro** (oxtail stew) and **salmorejo** (thicker, richer cousin of gazpacho). Both are local specialities.
days.gem: The Mezquita is free entry every morning from 8:30-9:30am for Catholic mass — you can enter as a visitor during this time at no cost.

days.title: Córdoba → Granada
days.morning: **Medina Azahara** — a ruined 10th-century caliphate city just outside Córdoba, largely unvisited. Drive there before leaving (30min from centre).
days.afternoon: Drive to **Granada** (200km, 2h). Check in, then walk up to the **Mirador de San Nicolás** for the classic Alhambra view at golden hour.
days.evening: Dinner in the **Albaicín** — Granada's Moorish quarter. Moroccan tea houses and tapas bars coexist on the same steep streets.
days.gem: Granada is the last city in Spain where tapas still come free with every drink — order a beer and get a full plate of food at no extra cost.

days.title: The Alhambra
days.theme: the most visited monument in Spain
days.morning: **Alhambra Palace** — book 6-8 weeks in advance, tickets sell out completely. Arrive at opening (8:30am). Start with the Nasrid Palaces (timed entry slot).
days.afternoon: **Generalife gardens** and the Alcazaba fortress are included in the ticket and less crowded in the afternoon. Take your time — most people rush.
days.evening: Flamenco in a **cave tablaos** in Sacromonte neighbourhood — the authentic Roma flamenco tradition of Granada, different in style from Seville.
days.gem: The Partal gardens at the east end of the Alhambra are the oldest surviving part and almost always empty — a reflecting pool with a porticoed tower.

days.title: Granada → Ronda
days.theme: the white village on the cliff
days.morning: Drive to **Ronda** (180km, 2h via A-92 and A-374). Ronda sits on a dramatic gorge — arrival is genuinely breathtaking.
days.afternoon: **Puente Nuevo** bridge over the 100m El Tajo gorge. Walk down into the gorge via the Camino de los Molinos. **Plaza de Toros** — one of Spain's oldest bullrings, now a museum.
days.evening: Stay overnight in Ronda — smaller than the other cities but the evening atmosphere when day-trippers leave is magical.
days.gem: The path down to the old Arab baths at the bottom of the gorge takes 20 minutes and is almost always empty — the view back up to the bridge is the best angle.

days.title: Ronda → Málaga & Departure
days.morning: Drive Ronda to **Málaga** (100km, 1h20). Return rental car at AGP airport. If time allows: **Picasso Museum** in Málaga city centre (he was born here).
days.afternoon: **Málaga Cathedral** and the Alcazaba fortress — smaller than Granada's but far less crowded. Walk the **Calle Larios** pedestrian street.
days.evening: Flight home from Málaga (AGP). Last espresso and a **pestiño** pastry at the airport.
days.gem: Málaga's Soho neighbourhood has become one of Spain's best street art districts in the last 5 years — a 30-minute walk reveals murals by international artists.
